prs.title: (feat) O3-4217: Explicitly define all dependencies in distro properties
prs.body:
## Requirements
- [x] This PR has a title that briefly describes the work done including the ticket number. If there is a ticket, make sure your PR title includes a conventional commit label. See existing PR titles for inspiration.
- [ ] My work is based on designs, which are linked or shown either in the Jira ticket or the description below.
- [x] My work includes tests or is validated by existing tests.

## Summary
Fixes [O3-4217](https://openmrs.atlassian.net/browse/O3-4217)

As highlighted in SDK-360, the `distro.properties` file was missing several explicit dependency declarations, relying on separate files and Dockerfile ARGs instead of being the single source of truth for the distribution.

Changes made:
- Added `omod.spa=${spa.version}` to both `distro.properties` and `distro-no-demo.properties` — the SPA backend OMOD was not declared at all
- Added `spa.core=next` — app shell version was previously only set via `ARG APP_SHELL_VERSION=next` in `frontend/Dockerfile`
- Added all 44 `spa.frontendModules.@openmrs/esm-*=next` entries — frontend modules were previously only defined in `frontend/spa-assemble-config.json`
- Added `<spa.version>2.1.0</spa.version>` version property and `spa-omod` Maven dependency in `pom.xml`

`frontend/spa-assemble-config.json` and `frontend/Dockerfile` are intentionally left unchanged. Version pinning for frontend modules will be addressed in a follow-up.

## Screenshots
N/A — no UI changes.

## Related Issue
[O3-4217](https://openmrs.atlassian.net/browse/O3-4217)

## Other
N/A

prs.title: (fix) RESTWS-1035: Deduplicate concept results in memberOf and fuzzy search paths
prs.body:
## Description of what I changed

The `doSearch()` method in `ConceptResource1_8.java` and the fuzzy search path in `ConceptSearchHandler1_8.java` never deduplicated their results lists. Both `getConceptsByConceptSet()` and `getConcepts()` can return duplicate `Concept` objects due to Hibernate JOIN FETCH on multi-valued associations (`concept_name`, `concept_map`), causing the REST response to contain the same concept multiple times.

### Root Cause
- `getConceptsByConceptSet()` uses an HQL query that joins on the `concept_set` table and can produce duplicate rows when a concept has multiple names or mappings
- `getConcepts()` similarly produces duplicates from joins on the `concept_name` table
- Neither the `memberOf`, `answerTo`, nor `q` search paths ever deduplicated the final results

### Changes Made

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `ConceptResource1_8.java` | Added `LinkedHashSet` deduplication after results list is built, covering `memberOf`, `answerTo`, and `q` search paths |
| `ConceptSearchHandler1_8.java` | Added the same `LinkedHashSet` dedup in the `fuzzy` searchType path |
| `ConceptController1_8Test.java` | Added `doSearch_shouldNotReturnDuplicateMembersOfConcept()` to assert no duplicate UUIDs are returned when querying `memberOf` |

### Why LinkedHashSet?
`LinkedHashSet` eliminates duplicates while preserving insertion order, making it the safest and most localized fix no upstream query behavior is changed.

## Issue I worked on
see https://issues.openmrs.org/browse/RESTWS-1035

## Checklist: I completed these to help reviewers :)
- [x] My IDE is configured to follow the [**code style**](https://wiki.openmrs.org/display/docs/Java+Conventions) of this project.
- [x] I have **added tests** to cover my changes.
- [x] I ran `mvn clean package` right before creating this pull request and added all formatting changes to my commit.
- [ ] All new and existing **tests passed**.
- [x] My pull request is **based on the latest changes** of the master branch.

prs.title: (fix) O3-5631: Guard null on encounters data access in patient registration hooks
prs.body:
## Requirements

* [x] This PR has a title that briefly describes the work done
* [x] My work is based on designs, which are linked or shown either in the Jira ticket or the description below
* [ ] My work includes tests or is validated by existing tests

## Summary

Fixes a null dereference bug in patient-registration-hooks.ts where `data?.data.results` only guarded `data` but not `data.data`. If the API returns a malformed or empty response, `data.data` can be undefined, causing a TypeError that crashes the entire patient edit form.

Fix applied: `data?.data.results` → `data?.data?.results`

## Screenshots
N/A — no UI changes.

## Related Issue
https://openmrs.atlassian.net/browse/O3-5631

## Other
This change improves stability by preventing crashes when API responses are malformed or incomplete.

prs.title: fix: rules page, text selection & hamburger icon
prs.body: ## Summary This PR refines the UI, improves navigation consistency, and updates content on the Rules page across the SkillFest platform. --- ## Changes ### UI & Styling - **Text selection** — Updated global `::selection` styling to use black background with white text - **Hamburger menu** — Fixed hamburger icon visibility and behavior on small screens ### Rules Page - Built out the full Rules page with all 5 sections — Eligibility, Participation Rules, Scoring System, Code of Conduct, and Disqualification - Added scoring table with all 5 difficulty tiers (Beginner Friendly → Extreme) and point values - Added info callout in Section 01 clarifying that NST-SDC Developer Club applications are handled exclusively via the Fresher Track --- ## Type of Change - [x] Bug fix - [x] UI improvement - [ ] New feature - [x] Content update ## Summary by Sourcery Update navigation and layout to support a new Rules page while refining selection styling and scroll behavior. New Features: - Add a dedicated Rules page outlining eligibility, participation, scoring, code of conduct, and disqualification policies for SkillFest. - Introduce an automatic scroll-to-top behavior on route changes across the app. Bug Fixes: - Improve navbar link and hamburger icon visibility and behavior on scroll and mobile. - Ensure footer platform links scroll the page back to the top when navigating. Enhancements: - Add Rules entry to the main navbar and refine mobile drawer spacing. - Update footer social links to point to GitHub and support email, and simplify the footer’s lower section layout. - Change global text selection styling to use a high-contrast black background with white text for better readability. Documentation: - Document event rules and guidelines directly in the UI via the new Rules page. ##Changed Images made hamburger icon visible for the mobile screens. Added Rules in navigation bar Added Rules Page made changes in footer redirection links

prs.title: (fix) O3-4643: Skip applying form translations when locale doesn't match
prs.body:
## Requirements
- [x] This PR has a title that briefly describes the work done including the ticket number. If there is a ticket, make sure your PR title includes a [conventional commit](https://o3-dev.docs.openmrs.org/#/getting_started/contributing?id=your-pr-title-should-indicate-the-type-of-change-it-is) label. See existing PR titles for inspiration.
- [x] My work conforms to the [OpenMRS 3.0 Styleguide](https://om.rs/styleguide) and [design documentation](https://zeroheight.com/23a080e38/p/880723-introduction).
- [x] My work includes tests or is validated by existing tests.

## Summary
When a form has a translation file for one language (e.g. French) but not for the user's current locale (e.g. English), the French translations were incorrectly applied to the form — because there was no locale check before calling `addResourceBundle`.

This fix:
- Retrieves the session locale via `useSession()` with fallback to `window.i18next.language`
- Normalizes to language code only (e.g. `en_GB` → `en`) to handle region variants
- Only calls `addResourceBundle` if `formJson.translations.language` matches the current locale
- Forms with no matching translation file now fall back gracefully to their original labels

## Screenshots
N/A: no UI changes.

## Related Issue
https://issues.openmrs.org/browse/O3-4643

## Other
Reference workaround for the same issue: https://github.com/openmrs/path-drc-emr/pull/12

prs.title: (fix) O3-5584: Await wardPatientGroupDetails.mutate() before resetting submit state in patient transfer form
prs.body:
## Requirements
- [x] This PR has a title that briefly describes the work done including the ticket number.
- [x] My work includes tests or is validated by existing tests.

## Summary

In `patient-admit-or-transfer-request-form.component.tsx`, the `finally` block had two issues:

1. `wardPatientGroupDetails.mutate()` was called without `await` — SWR cache invalidation fired and was forgotten, causing unreliable ward list re-renders and potential unhandled rejections if the component unmounted mid-mutation.

2. `setIsSubmitting(false)` ran before `mutate()` resolved — the submit button re-enabled while the mutation was still in-flight, allowing a user to submit again before the ward list refreshed and risking duplicate transfer requests.

The correct pattern already exists in `admit-patient-button.component.tsx` in the same repo. This component had diverged from it.

**Fix:** Changed the `finally` block to await the mutation before resetting submit state:
```ts
.finally(async () => {
  await wardPatientGroupDetails?.mutate?.();
  setIsSubmitting(false);
});
```

## Screenshots

N/A — no UI changes.

## Related Issue

https://openmrs.atlassian.net/browse/O3-5584

## Other

No existing test file for this component. The fix is verifiable by comparison with the correct pattern already used in `admit-patient-button.component.tsx` in the same repo.

prs.title: (fix) O3-5582: Await handler.sync() to correctly capture async errors in syncDynamicOfflineData
prs.body:
## Requirements
- [x] This PR has a title that briefly describes the work done including the ticket number.
- [x] My work includes tests or is validated by existing tests.

## Summary

In `dynamic-offline-data.ts`, `handler.sync(identifier, abortSignal)` was called without `await`. Since `sync()` returns `Promise<void>`, async rejections were never caught by the surrounding `try/catch`, causing `syncState` to always report all handlers as succeeded — even when sync failed.

This meant `syncState.erroredHandlers` was always empty and the syncState written to IndexedDB was always incorrect. Rejected promises escaped to `onunhandledrejection`, producing a generic error toast with no actionable information about which handler or data entry failed.

**Fix:** Added `await` before `handler.sync()`.

**Test:** Added a regression test in `dynamic-offline-data.test.ts` that registers a handler whose `sync()` rejects, calls `syncDynamicOfflineData`, and asserts the handler appears in `erroredHandlers` and not in `succeededHandlers`. This test fails on the pre-fix code, confirming the bug was real.

## Screenshots

N/A — no UI changes.

## Related Issue

https://openmrs.atlassian.net/browse/O3-5582

## Other

N/A

prs.title: (feat) O3-5570: Add parallel RFE schema rendering path alongside existing AFE pipeline
prs.body:
## Summary
This is a proof-of-concept implementation demonstrating that HFE XML can be  converted into a valid React Form Engine (RFE) JSON schema alongside the  existing Angular Form Engine (AFE) pipeline — without modifying any existing logic.

## Motivation
The current converter outputs AFE schema exclusively. As OpenMRS O3 adoption grows, implementers with large HFE form libraries need a path to RFE-compatible schemas. This PoC validates that the conversion is technically feasible within 
the existing architecture.

## What is implemented
`HtmlFormRfeObsRenderer` — converts `HtmlFormDataPoint` → RFE question node
`HtmlFormRfeObsGroupRenderer` — converts `<obsgroup>` element → RFE group node
`RfeSchemaBuilder` — assembles questions into valid RFE top-level structure
`rfeMode` flag in `HtmlFormUtil` — existing AFE path is completely unchanged

## What is NOT in scope (intentional)
Conditional logic (`hideWhenExpression`, repeat groups)
Full rendering type coverage
CLI mode
Production hardening

## Example
Input: `examples/sample-hfe.xml`
Output: `examples/sample-rfe-output.json`

## Notes
This PR is part of GSoC 2026 proposal work for the Forms Migration Tool project.
Feedback on the approach and insertion point is welcome.

prs.title: (fix) O3-3348: Show check-in button for patients with an active visit
prs.body:
## Requirements
- [x] This PR has a title that briefly describes the work done including the ticket number. If there is a ticket, make sure your PR title includes a conventional commit label. See existing PR titles for inspiration.
- [x] My work is based on designs, which are linked or shown either in the Jira ticket or the description below.
- [x] My work includes tests or is validated by existing tests.

## Summary
Fixes a workflow gap where patients who already have an active visit could not be checked in to their scheduled appointment.

The system previously assumed "Check In" and "Start a Visit" were the same action. This broke the workflow when a patient was already present at the clinic for another reason (e.g. emergency visit, different department) and also had a scheduled appointment.

**Before this fix:**
- Patients with an active visit could not check in to their appointment
- The appointment remained stuck in `Expected` state with no way to transition it

**After this fix:**
- If the patient has NO active visit → existing behavior unchanged (Start Visit workspace opens)
- If the patient HAS an active visit → appointment status is updated to `CheckedIn` directly via API, no duplicate visit is created
- The appointments list auto-refreshes after a successful check-in

**Files changed:**
- `appointments-actions.component.tsx`: pass `hasActiveVisit` and `mutateAppointments` props to `CheckInButton`
- `checkin-button.component.tsx`: conditional click handler based on active visit state + auto-refresh on success
- `appointments-actions.test.tsx`: added test cases for active visit scenarios
- `translations/en.json`: added translation strings for snackbar messages

## Screenshots
this is before the fix

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/534a3492-85d2-4acb-8024-67340ffeed70

after the fix

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/353973dc-5f9a-41a4-8cf0-a58ff10481e0

## Related Issue
https://issues.openmrs.org/browse/O3-3348

## Other
- The previous declined PR #1304 only changed button visibility. This PR addresses the underlying check-in behavior for active visits.
- `useTodaysVisits` has a pre-existing limitation noted in a TODO comment: visits that didn't start today or inpatient visits may not be detected correctly. This is a separate concern outside the scope of O3-3348.
- Check Out behavior (whether it should also end the active visit) is intentionally out of scope and warrants its own ticket.

prs.title: (chore) O3-5524: Memoize AST parsing in extension display expression evaluator
prs.body:
## Requirements
- [x] This PR has a title that briefly describes the work done including the ticket number.
- [ ] My work is based on designs, which are linked or shown either in the Jira ticket or the description below.
- [x] My work includes tests or is validated by existing tests.
- [ ] I have updated the esm-framework and storybook mocks to reflect any API changes.

## Summary

The extension `displayExpression` evaluator calls `jsep()` to  parse expression strings into ASTs on every evaluation. Since 
`updateExtensionOutputStore` re-evaluates all display expressions  across all slots on every global store update, the same expression strings are parsed repeatedly — once per extension per store update.

This PR adds a module-level `Map` cache so each unique expression string is parsed into an AST exactly once. Subsequent evaluations reuse the cached AST directly, eliminating redundant CPU work.

**Before:** Every call to `evaluate()` → `jsep(expression)` parses the string from scratch

**After:** First call → parse and cache. Subsequent calls → return cached AST instantly

**Changed files:**
- `packages/framework/esm-expression-evaluator/src/evaluator.ts`
- `packages/framework/esm-expression-evaluator/src/evaluator.test.ts`

## Screenshots

Not applicable — performance improvement with no UI changes.

## Related Issue

https://openmrs.atlassian.net/browse/O3-5524

## Other

This is a performance prerequisite for the GSoC 2026 project: "Dynamic EHR: Custom screens based on user roles, locations and other values."

Dynamic EHR requires frequent context updates (selecting a visit, changing location, opening an encounter). Without AST 
memoization, each context change triggers O(N*M) string parsing across all slots and extensions — causing main thread blocking as the number of context-aware extensions grows.

The cache is intentionally module-scoped and long-lived since expression strings are static (defined in routes.json) and 
never change at runtime.

prs.title: (fix) O3-5523: Add deep equality check on ExtensionSlot state prop to prevent cascading re-renders
prs.body:
## Requirements
- [x] This PR has a title that briefly describes the work done including the ticket number.
- [ ] My work is based on designs, which are linked or shown either in the Jira ticket or the description below.
- [x] My work includes tests or is validated by existing tests.
- [ ] I have updated the esm-framework and storybook mocks to reflect any API changes.


## Summary

`useExtensionSlot` uses a `useEffect` with `[slotName, state]` in its dependency array. When a parent component passes `state` as an inline object:

The `useEffect` fires on every parent render because inline objects always fail JavaScript reference equality. This causes   `updateExtensionSlotState` to dispatch on every render, forcing `extensionInternalStore` to update globally and triggering   `updateExtensionOutputStore` across all extension slots — an expensive operation for what should be a no-op.

This PR introduces a **deep equality check using `lodash-es/isEqual`** to ensure that updates are only dispatched when the actual content of `state` changes, not just its reference.

Additionally:
- `prevStateRef` now stores the raw state instead of a serialized version  
- `prevStateRef` is initialized lazily (after the first effect run) to avoid unnecessary comparisons  

This approach also correctly handles non-serializable values (e.g., functions) passed via `state`, which would not work reliably with `JSON.stringify`.


## Changed files
- `packages/framework/esm-react-utils/src/useExtensionSlot.ts`
- `packages/framework/esm-react-utils/src/extensions.test.tsx`


## Screenshots

Not applicable — no UI changes.


## Related Issue

https://openmrs.atlassian.net/browse/O3-5523


## Other

As more context variables (location, visit, encounter) are injected via `ExtensionSlot` state props, this issue would cause unnecessary cascading re-renders across extension slots. Fixing it improves performance and ensures the system scales efficiently.

prs.title: (feat) O3-5522: Add hasPrivilege helper to extension display expression context
prs.body:
## Requirements
- [x] This PR has a title that briefly describes the work done including the ticket number.
- [ ] My work is based on designs, which are linked or shown either in the Jira ticket or the description below.
- [x] My work includes tests or is validated by existing tests.
- [ ] I have updated the esm-framework and storybook mocks to reflect any API changes.

## Summary
The extension `displayExpression` system supports arbitrary JavaScript expressions, but writing privilege-based conditions required verbose syntax:
```javascript
// Before — verbose and error-prone
"displayExpression": "session.user.privileges.some(p => p.display === 'Edit Orders')"
```

This PR adds a helper function to the expression evaluator context:

- `hasPrivilege(privName)` — returns true if the current user has the named privilege

Implementers can now write clean, readable conditions in `routes.json`:
```json
{ "displayExpression": "hasPrivilege('Edit Orders')" }
```

The helper uses optional chaining and returns a boolean, so existing extensions remain unaffected.

**Changed files:**
- `packages/framework/esm-extensions/src/extensions.ts`
- `packages/framework/esm-extensions/src/extensions.test.ts`

## Screenshots
Not applicable — framework logic with no UI changes.

## Related Issue
https://openmrs.atlassian.net/browse/O3-5522

## Other
This PR focuses on improving readability of privilege-based display conditions.

prs.title: (test) O3-5521: Add tests for extension display condition evaluation
prs.body:
## Requirements
- [x] This PR has a title that briefly describes the work done including the ticket number. Ensure your PR title includes a conventional commit label (such as `feat`, `fix`, or `chore`, among others). See existing PR titles for inspiration.

## If applicable
- [ ] My work is based on designs, which are linked or shown either in he Jira ticket or the description below.
- [x] My work includes tests or is validated by existing tests.
- [ ] I have updated the esm-framework and storybook mocks (mock.tsx, mock-jest.tsx, and storybook mocks) to reflect any API changes.

## Summary

The extension condition evaluation pipeline in `getAssignedExtensionsFromSlotData()` had zero test coverage despite 
being the most complex and critical logic in the extension system.  This function evaluates four condition types in sequence privilege checks, display expressions, feature flags, and online/offline status before determining which extensions appear in a slot.

This PR adds 14 tests covering all condition types:

- Privilege-based show/hide including System Developer bypass
- Display expression evaluation with session context
- Malformed expression error handling and logging
- Feature flag gating (enabled and disabled)
- Combined condition scenarios (all pass / one fails)

**Changed files:**
- `packages/framework/esm-extensions/src/extensions.test.ts`

## Screenshots

Not applicable — test-only change.

## Related Issue

https://openmrs.atlassian.net/browse/O3-5521

## Other

This test coverage is a prerequisite for safely extending the condition system as part of the GSoC 2026 project: "Dynamic EHR: Custom screens based on user roles, locations and other values."

The tests document the existing contract and will serve as a regression baseline when new condition types (location tags, visit context, helper functions) are added.

Note: The pre-push hook reported a pre-existing failure in `@openmrs/esm-login-app` unrelated to this change. All 50 tests 
in `@openmrs/esm-extensions` pass (36 existing + 14 new).

prs.title: (fix) O3-5517: Apply graphOldestFirst config to FHIR sort order in useObs
prs.body:
## Requirements

- [x] This PR has a title that briefly describes the work done including 
      the ticket number.
- [ ] My work is based on designs, which are linked or shown either in 
      the Jira ticket or the description below.
- [x] My work includes tests or is validated by existing tests.

## Summary

The `useObs` hook had an explicit TODO to make sorting respect the `graphOldestFirst` config option. The hook always used `_sort=-date` (newest first) regardless of config. The workaround in `obs-graph.component.tsx` applied a client-side `.reverse()` on the already-paginated result, meaning patients with >100 observations received the 100 most recent entries in reverse — not the actual oldest data.

This fix moves sort direction server-side into the FHIR query and removes the now-redundant client-side reversal.

**Changed files:**
- `packages/esm-generic-patient-widgets-app/src/resources/useObs.ts`
- `packages/esm-generic-patient-widgets-app/src/obs-graph/obs-graph.component.tsx`

## Screenshots

Not applicable — no UI layout change.

## Related Issue

https://openmrs.atlassian.net/browse/O3-5517

## Other

This fix resolves an explicit TODO left in the codebase by a previous developer. It is also a prerequisite for the O3 Growth Chart GSoC project — growth charts must display weight observations from birth onward with the full observation history. The previous client-side reversal of a truncated result set made this impossible for patients with >100 observations.

prs.title: (fix) O3-5515: Remove hardcoded 10-point data limit from vitals and biometrics charts
prs.body:
## Requirements

- [x] This PR has a title that briefly describes the work done including the ticket number. If there is a ticket, make sure your PR title  includes a conventional commit label. See existing PR titles for inspiration.
- [ ] My work is based on designs, which are linked or shown either in the Jira ticket or the description below. (See also: Styleguide)
- [x] My work includes tests or is validated by existing tests.

## Summary
The vitals and biometrics chart components previously hardcoded .slice(0, 10) in their useMemo blocks, silently discarding all observations beyond the 10 most recent. For a pediatric patient seen monthly over 2 years, this means 14 of 24 weight measurements are never shown.

This PR replaces that arbitrary count-based truncation with a 12-month default time window, keeping charts focused on clinically relevant recent data while no longer silently dropping older observations. The existing Carbon Charts zoom bar `(zoomBar: { top: { enabled: true } })` allows users to navigate the full history when needed.

What changed

Removed `.slice(0, 10)` from vitals-chart.component.tsx and biometrics-chart.component.tsx
Added a cutoff of 12 months (via useMemo) so charts default to recent data
cutoff is stable across renders and included in the chartData dependency array
What this is not This is a standalone fix and is not a prerequisite for or dependency of any growth chart work. The growth chart is a separate app and will manage its own data range logic independently.

Testing No dedicated unit tests exist for these chart components (confirmed via grep). TypeScript `(tsc --noEmit) `and ESLint both pass clean after this change.



## Screenshots

N/A — no UI layout changes. The chart now renders all data points instead of silently capping at 10. The zoom bar handles navigation across the full history.

## Related Issue

https://issues.openmrs.org/browse/O3-3

## Other

No tests exist for `vitals-chart.component.tsx` or 
`biometrics-chart.component.tsx` (confirmed via grep). TypeScript  (`tsc --noEmit`) and ESLint both pass clean after this change.
Note on performance: Both charts already have Carbon Charts' built-in  zoom bar enabled (`zoomBar: { top: { enabled: true } }`). This gives users a native pan/zoom control for navigating large datasets without  rendering all points at once. For patients with extremely large observation histories, the zoom bar provides the appropriate UX — the hardcoded limit was not the right solution for that concern.

prs.title: (fix) O3-5501: Replace duplicate ErrorState component with re-export from framework
prs.body:
## Requirements

- [x] This PR has a title that briefly describes the work done including the ticket number. If there is a ticket, make sure your PR title includes a [conventional commit](https://o3-docs.openmrs.org/docs/frontend-modules/contributing.en-US#contributing-guidelines) label. See existing PR titles for inspiration.
- [ ] My work is based on designs, which are linked or shown either in the Jira ticket or the description below. (See also: [Styleguide](http://om.rs/o3ui))
- [x] My work includes tests or is validated by existing tests.

## Summary
Removes the duplicate ErrorState component from esm-patient-common-lib and migrates all consumers to use the canonical version re-exported from @openmrs/esm-framework (originally from esm-styleguide).

This was requested by @denniskigen in #3090 and agreed by @ibacher. The local version was redundant — @openmrs/esm-framework already exposes an identical ErrorState component. The only difference is that the framework version uses error: any instead of error: FetchError | Error, which is acceptable per maintainer guidance.

### Changes:

- Removed the duplicate ErrorState component, styles, and unit test from esm-patient-common-lib
- Replaced it with a thin re-export shim (`export { ErrorState, type ErrorStateProps } from '@openmrs/esm-framework'`) so the public API is preserved and external consumers are unaffected
- Updated 16 test files to assert on ErrorState mock props instead of rendered output, since the re-export now resolves to the framework's jest mock in tests

## Screenshots

## Related Issue
https://issues.openmrs.org/browse/O3-5501

## Other
Supersedes #3090 (which is now closed).

prs.title: (feat) O3-5484: Add daily/weekly/monthly calendar view switcher
prs.body: ## Requirements - [x] This PR has a title that briefly describes the work done including the ticket number and uses a conventional commit label. - [ ] My work is based on designs, which are linked or shown either in the Jira ticket or the description below. - [x] My work includes tests or is validated by existing tests. ## Summary The appointments calendar only rendered the MonthlyCalendarView even though the useAppointmentsCalendar hook already supports daily, weekly, and monthly periods. Additionally, clicking a day navigated away from the calendar entirely, breaking the user's workflow. This PR makes two improvements: 1. Adds a ContentSwitcher allowing users to toggle between Daily, Weekly, and Monthly views. The selected view is tracked in the global appointments store. Monthly renders the existing calendar. Daily and weekly show placeholders with TODO comments marking them as future work. 2. Replaces page navigation on day click with a modal showing appointments for that date, keeping users in their calendar context. ## Screenshots ## Related Issue https://issues.openmrs.org/browse/O3-5484 ## Other - Zero breaking changes — monthly remains the default view - npx tsc --noEmit passes with 0 errors - Existing test suite passes

prs.title: (fix) O3-5475: Type ErrorState error prop as FetchError instead of any
prs.body:
## Requirements
- [x] This PR has a title that briefly describes the work done including the ticket number and     uses a conventional commit label.
- [ ] My work is based on designs, which are linked or shown either in the Jira ticket or the      description below.
- [x] My work includes tests or is validated by existing tests.

## Summary
The `ErrorState` component in `esm-patient-common-lib` had its `error` prop typed as `any`. The component's own JSDoc already states the error is "Expected to be a failed fetch result", and it accesses `error?.response?.status` and `error?.response?.statusText` internally — which matches the `FetchError` interface exported from `@openmrs/esm-framework`.

This PR replaces `error: any` with `error: FetchError`, enforcing the intended contract at compile time without changing any runtime behavior. The test mock is updated to include `responseBody: null` to satisfy the `FetchError` interface shape.

## Screenshots
N/A — no UI changes.

## Related Issue
https://issues.openmrs.org/browse/O3-5475

## Other
Verification:
- `npx tsc --noEmit` → 0 errors in esm-patient-common-lib
- `npx jest src/error-state/error-state.test.tsx` → 1 passed
- Zero runtime behavior changes

prs.title: (chore) O3-5467: Fix strict-mode type errors in help component
prs.body:
# Requirements

- [x] This PR has a title that briefly describes the work done including the ticket number and uses a conventional commit label.

## If applicable

- [ ] My work is based on designs, which are linked or shown either in the Jira ticket or the description below.
- [x] My work is validated by existing tests.
- [ ] I have updated the esm-framework mocks (mock.tsx and mock-jest.tsx) to reflect any API changes.

---

## Summary

This PR resolves several TypeScript strict-mode errors in `help.component.tsx` within `esm-help-menu-app`, identified while evaluating strict compatibility for O3 app packages.

The changes ensure the component compiles successfully under `--strict` without affecting runtime behavior.

### Changes

**Untyped refs**
`useRef(null)` infers `MutableRefObject<null>` under strict mode, which prevents safe `.contains()` checks.
Fixed by adding explicit element types:

```ts
const buttonRef = useRef<HTMLButtonElement>(null);
const menuRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
```
Incorrect event handler typing
handleClickOutside was typed as MouseEvent but registered on both mousedown and touchstart.
Since touchstart emits TouchEvent, strict mode reports a type mismatch.
Updated the handler signature to:

```ts
(event: MouseEvent | TouchEvent)
```

event.target typing
event.target is typed as EventTarget | null, while .contains() requires Node | null.
Resolved by safely casting:

```ts
const target = event.target as Node | null;
```

These changes allow the component to compile cleanly under strict TypeScript settings while preserving existing runtime behavior.

Screenshots

N/A — no UI changes.

Related Issue

https://issues.openmrs.org/browse/O3-5467

## Testing

The following checks were performed:

- `npx tsc --noEmit --strict` → 0 errors in `esm-help-menu-app`
- `yarn turbo run build --filter=@openmrs/esm-help-menu-app` → build succeeds
- `yarn workspace @openmrs/esm-help-menu-app test` → all tests passing

No runtime behaviour changes were introduced.

prs.title: O3-5462: chore(esm-utils): enable strict mode as TypeScript migration pilot
prs.body:
# Requirements

- [x] This PR has a title that briefly describes the work done including the ticket number. Ensure your PR title includes a conventional commit label (such as `feat`, `fix`, or `chore`, among others). See existing PR titles for inspiration.

## If applicable

- [ ] My work is based on designs, which are linked or shown either in the Jira ticket or the description below.
- [x] My work includes tests or is validated by existing tests.
- [ ] I have updated the esm-framework mocks (mock.tsx and mock-jest.tsx) to reflect any API changes.

---

## Summary

This PR enables `"strict": true` in `@openmrs/esm-utils` as a pilot step toward incremental strict TypeScript migration across OpenMRS O3 frontend packages.

Enabling strict mode surfaced 6 type errors across 3 files. All surfaced issues were internal and have been resolved without introducing suppressions or altering runtime behavior.

### Why `esm-utils`?

- Shared foundational package used across multiple O3 microfrontends.
- Small enough to validate migration workflow safely.
- Does not expose externally versioned API contracts.
- Errors were meaningful but manageable in a single reviewable PR.

### Errors surfaced and resolved

**retry.ts (3 issues)**
- Added explicit `: boolean` return type to `shouldRetry` (previously implicitly `any`).
- Updated `onError?(e: any)` → `onError?(e: unknown)` to reflect that thrown values in JavaScript are not guaranteed to be `Error`.
- Updated `let lastError: any` → `unknown` for the same reason.

**shallowEqual.ts (1 issue)**
- Introduced `Record<string, unknown>` cast after object narrowing to comply with strict index access rules.

**test-helpers.ts (2 issues)**
- Typed `tmp` as `Record<string, unknown>` instead of `{}`.
- Updated `isOrdinaryObject(x: any)` → `(x: unknown)` while preserving existing narrowing behavior.

### Safety

- No runtime logic changes.
- No exported API contracts were narrowed.
- No `@ts-ignore` or `@ts-expect-error` suppressions introduced.
- All existing tests pass unchanged.
- Local workspace build completed successfully to verify no downstream type regressions.

### Migration Pattern Demonstrated

This PR validates the incremental strict migration approach:

1. Enable `"strict": true` at the package level.
2. Surface strict-mode violations.
3. Fix internal issues correctly (no suppressions).
4. Ensure no consumer breakage.
5. Verify downstream compatibility.

This pattern can be applied package-by-package across the O3 frontend to progressively strengthen type safety.

---

## Screenshots

N/A — No UI changes.

---

## Related Issue

https://issues.openmrs.org/browse/O3-5462

---

## Other

This PR serves as a pilot to evaluate strict-mode feasibility on a shared utility package before expanding migration to additional frontend modules.

State updates triggered by window.resize events.
Validation of isDesktop logic for all layout variants.
No production code changes were made. This PR strictly improves test coverage and validates existing behavior.

afterEach cleanup ensures no class leakage between test cases.

prs.title: fix(styleguide): defensively handle arbitrary thrown values in ErrorState
prs.body:
# Requirements

- [x] This PR has a title that briefly describes the work done including the ticket number. Ensure your PR title includes a conventional commit label (such as `feat`, `fix`, or `chore`, among others).

## If applicable

- [ ] My work is based on designs, which are linked or shown either in the Jira ticket or the description below.
- [x] My work includes tests or is validated by existing tests.
- [ ] I have updated the esm-framework mocks (mock.tsx and mock-jest.tsx) to reflect any API changes.

## Summary

This PR improves the runtime resilience of the `ErrorState` component by defensively handling arbitrary JavaScript throw values.

Previously, `ErrorState` assumed errors followed a fetch-style shape (`error.response.status` and `error.response.statusText`). However, JavaScript allows any value to be thrown (e.g. `Error` instances, strings, arbitrary objects, or `null`).

This change introduces a small helper (`getErrorDetail`) that safely extracts a displayable message from common error shapes without introducing stricter typing or modifying the public API.

Supported cases now include:
- Fetch-style errors (`{ response: { status, statusText } }`)
- `Error` instances
- String errors
- Arbitrary objects
- `null` / `undefined`

No changes were made to `ErrorStateProps`, imports, or external API behavior.

## Screenshots

No visual design changes were made. This update only improves runtime handling of different error shapes.

## Related Issue

N/A

## Other

This change follows maintainer guidance to improve runtime defensiveness rather than introducing stricter TypeScript typing for thrown values.

prs.title: fix(styleguide): translate patient contact attribute labels using stable key
prs.body:
# Requirements

- [x] This PR has a title that briefly describes the work done including the ticket number. Ensure your PR title includes a [conventional commit](https://o3-docs.openmrs.org/docs/frontend-modules/contributing#contributing-guidelines) label (such as `feat`, `fix`, or `chore`, among others). See existing PR titles for inspiration.

## If applicable

- [ ] My work is based on designs, which are linked or shown either in the Jira ticket or the description below.
- [x] My work includes tests or is validated by existing tests.
- [ ] I have updated the esm-framework mocks ([mock.tsx](https://github.com/openmrs/openmrs-esm-core/blob/main/packages/framework/esm-framework/mock.tsx) and [mock-jest.tsx](https://github.com/openmrs/openmrs-esm-core/blob/main/packages/framework/esm-framework/mock-jest.tsx)) to reflect any API changes.

## Summary
This PR fixes an internationalization issue in the patient banner contact details component where backend-provided display values were being used as translation keys.

Previously, dynamic attributeType.display values caused contact labels (e.g. “Phone Number”) to remain untranslated in non-English locales.
This change switches to using the stable attributeType.name as the translation key, adds translation extraction hints for common contact attributes, and preserves a safe fallback for custom or unknown attributes.

This resolves an existing TODO in the component and ensures patient contact labels are localized where possible without breaking backward compatibility.

## Screenshots
<!-- Required if you are making UI changes. -->

## Related Issue
N/A

## Other
This approach follows guidance discussed in the OpenMRS frontend (#openmrs3) channel and aligns with existing patterns for handling dynamic, backend-driven labels.

prs.title: dd comprehensive accessibility enhancements for WCAG 2.1 AA compliance
prs.body:
## 🎯 Accessibility Enhancements

This PR implements comprehensive accessibility improvements across the FreeDevTools application to ensure WCAG 2.1 AA compliance and a better user experience for all users.

### ✅ Changes Made

#### 1. Skip Links & Navigation
- Added skip links for main content, navigation, and search
- Improved keyboard navigation throughout the application

#### 2. ARIA Labels & Roles
- Added proper ARIA labels to all interactive elements
- Implemented semantic roles (banner, main, contentinfo, etc.)
- Enhanced screen reader support with descriptive labels

#### 3. Keyboard Navigation
- Added keyboard shortcuts for JSON tools (Ctrl/Cmd + Enter)
- Improved tab navigation between form controls
- Enhanced focus management for complex components

#### 4. Focus Indicators
- Added comprehensive focus styles in global.css
- Ensured all interactive elements have visible focus indicators
- Implemented proper focus ring styling

#### 5. Screen Reader Support
- Added live regions for dynamic content updates
- Implemented status messages with aria-live
- Enhanced error announcements with role="alert"

#### 6. Semantic HTML Improvements
- Improved heading hierarchy
- Added proper landmark roles
- Enhanced form labeling and relationships

### 🧪 Testing
- Keyboard navigation tested
- Screen reader compatibility verified
- Focus indicators validated
- ARIA implementation checked

### 📁 Files Modified
- `frontend/src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro`
- `frontend/src/components/Header.tsx`
- `frontend/src/components/SearchBar.tsx`
- `frontend/src/components/Footer.tsx`
- `frontend/src/components/theme/ThemeSwitcher.tsx`
- `frontend/src/components/ui/copy-button.tsx`
- `frontend/src/pages/t/json-[tool]/_JsonPrettifier.tsx`
- `frontend/src/styles/global.css`

### 🎯 Benefits
- WCAG 2.1 AA compliance
- Better accessibility for users with disabilities
- Enhanced keyboard navigation
- Improved screen reader experience
- Future-proof accessibility implementation

### 🔗 Related Issues
Closes #X (if applicable)

### 📋 Checklist
- [x] Accessibility improvements implemented
- [x] Keyboard navigation tested
- [x] Screen reader compatibility verified
- [x] Focus indicators working
- [x] ARIA labels properly implemented
- [x] Semantic HTML structure maintained

prs.title: CSS a11y: focus-visible, skip link, reduced-motion; notes grid auto-fit + hover contrast; modal helpers
prs.body:
Summary
- Base: add .skip-link + .visually-hidden utilities, focus-visible outline, reduced-motion + forced-colors fallbacks.
- Notes: responsive grid via auto-fit, keep white text on download-button hover, and add .modal.open + body.no-scroll helpers.

Scope
- Files: styling/base.css, styling/notes.css.
- No HTML/JS changes in this PR.

Verification
- Keyboard focus rings visible on buttons/links/inputs.
- Skip link visible on focus and targets #main-content.
- Notes grid collapses empty tracks on wide screens; hover maintains contrast.
- Modal can be toggled via class without inline styles.

Accessibility
- Respects prefers-reduced-motion and forced-colors.

prs.title: HTML-only: header/footer fragments + a11y improvements (Home & Notes)
prs.body:
Summary
- Convert header/footer into pure HTML fragments for safe component injection.
- Add "Skip to main content" link and landmark roles on pages.
- Improve search semantics (role=search, explicit labels) on Home and Notes.
- Add aria-live to typewriter and notes grid; mark decorative icons aria-hidden.
- Upgrade modal to accessible dialog (role=dialog, aria-modal, labelled/ described).

Scope
- Files: components/header.html, components/footer.html, pages/index.html, pages/notes.html
- No CSS or JS changes; class names and JS hooks preserved.

Verification
- Header and footer load via scripts/script.js; active link highlighting works.
- Mobile menu, theme toggle, and back-to-top still function.
- Notes search/filters and modal behavior unchanged functionally.
